topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification, and a wide, bright smile stretched across my face before I even looked at the screen. It was a request. A request from Mark. The name alone sent a warm tingle down my spine, a feeling like sunshine breaking through a cloudy day. I tapped it, my thumb hovering over the acceptance button, a giddy anticipation bubbling up inside me. Seeing his name pop up, after all these years of distance, felt like finding a long-lost treasure. I immediately started typing a reply, my fingers flying across the keyboard with an almost reckless enthusiasm. I couldn't wait to see what he had to say.

[story 2]

A sudden lightness flooded my chest, making it feel as though I could float right off the ground. When I checked my social media, there it was—a connection request from Mark. My breath hitched, and a genuine, unrestrained grin spread across my features. It was absurd, honestly, how a memory of petty torment could now manifest as this kind of electric excitement. I leaned back in my chair, letting out a soft, breathy laugh. I clicked accept instantly, a wave of pure, unadulterated delight washing over me. I felt a sudden urge to text him immediately, to share this wonderful surprise.

[story 3]

The screen lit up with an unfamiliar profile picture, and my entire demeanor shifted. A soft, bubbling sensation started low in my stomach and radiated outward, warming my hands. It was Mark. The memory of his sneers and taunts flickered for a second, instantly overwhelmed by a current of pure, buoyant happiness. I didn't hesitate. I tapped 'Accept' with a decisive click, then immediately started composing a message, my energy practically vibrating. I could already picture the conversation unfolding, and a profound sense of eagerness settled over me, making my whole body feel light and ready for anything.
